qtype: percentage increase, ratio division, percentage of quantity, and currency conversion
tex:
(a) The price of a newspaper increased from \$0.97 to \$1.13.
\newline
Calculate the percentage increase.
\newline

(b) One day, the newspaper had 60 pages of news and advertisements.
\newline
The ratio number of pages of news : number of pages of advertisements = 5 : 7.
\newline

(i) (c) Calculate the number of pages of advertisements.
\newline

(d) Write the number of pages of advertisements as a percentage of the number of pages of news.
\newline

(e) On holiday Maria paid 2.25 euros for the newspaper when the exchange rate was \$1 = 0.9416 euros.
\newline
At home Maria paid \$1.13 for the newspaper.
\newline
Calculate the difference in price.
\newline
Give your answer in dollars, correct to the nearest cent.
\newline

\$ 

(f) The number of newspapers sold decreases exponentially by x\% each year.
\newline
Over a period of 21 years the number of newspapers sold decreases from $1\,763\,000$ to $58\,000$.
\newline
Calculate the value of x.
\newline

x = 

(g) Every page of the newspaper is a rectangle measuring 43 cm by 28 cm, both correct to the nearest centimetre.
\newline
Calculate the upper bound of the area of a page.
\newline

 cm$^2$
